title.rendered: AMI Announces US Patent Awards for 2013

content.rendered:
NORCROSS, GEORGIA – Continuing its long tradition of excellence in BIOS, remote management and network storage innovations, AMI is pleased to announce its latest round of patent awards. AMI was awarded an impressive total of fifty U.S. patents during 2013. These awards bring the company closer to an important milestone of three hundred patents in total.

Notably, 29 engineers and developers from AMI, collaborating across the globe, were awarded patents this year. Several individuals were awarded multiple patents in 2013, ranging from four to six patents each for three individuals. The distribution of these awards across each of AMI’s engineering groups highlights the team-oriented atmosphere that has long been a part of AMI’s global corporate culture. Additionally, patents were granted in all of AMI’s core technology areas, including BIOS and UEFI Firmware, Remote and Server Management Technologies, Android Solutions and Network Data Storage and Storage Management.

“Our corporate strategy at AMI has always been to strengthen our core technologies, continue to expand our business activities into new markets and new technology areas and position ourselves favorably for the future. The achievements by this year’s AMI inventors reinforces this strategy, and as such I would like to commend our inventors – and all of the AMI staff collaborating with them – on helping contribute to a stronger, more responsive and future-oriented company. Their success also demonstrates that our corporate culture of innovation and teamwork across our network of international offices is a strong and effective one,” remarked Subramonian Shankar, President and CEO of AMI.

“My hope is that today’s announcement informs our customers and partners to continue to expect innovative research and development in the many fields in which AMI is engaged, and that their partnerships with AMI will continue to bear fruit for many years to come,” he added.
